name: psql
description: Run PostgreSQL queries and meta-commands via CLI
capabilities: Execute SQL queries against PostgreSQL databases
Execute SQL files for migrations and seed data
Output results in tuples-only, JSON, or formatted modes
Set query timeouts for long-running operations
author: Bind
securitySummary: Legitimate PostgreSQL query tool. Executes external psql commands and reads standard database credentials via environment variables. All 62 static findings are false positives stemming from pattern-matching without context. The scanner misidentified markdown code formatting as shell execution, PostgreSQL environment variables as sensitive data access, and documentation links as network indicators.
valueStatement: Database queries require switching between code editors and terminal windows. This skill lets you execute SQL queries and psql meta-commands directly from your AI assistant without leaving your workflow.
userTitle: Run PostgreSQL queries via command line
limitations: Requires psql CLI client installed separately
Requires bun runtime to execute queries
No persistent database connection between queries

useCases.title: Database schema exploration
useCases.description: List tables, describe table structures, and view indexes using meta-commands without switching to terminal.

useCases.title: Run analytical queries
useCases.description: Execute aggregation queries, COUNT operations, and GROUP BY statements to analyze database contents.

useCases.title: Database administration tasks
useCases.description: Check connection counts, table sizes, and run migration files to manage database operations.

promptTemplates.title: List all tables
promptTemplates.prompt: Use psql to list all tables in the database with \dt

promptTemplates.title: Describe table
promptTemplates.prompt: Show the structure of the users table using psql meta-commands

promptTemplates.title: Count records
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a query to count all records in the orders table where status equals pending

promptTemplates.title: Execute migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute the SQL file migrations/001_create_users.sql using the psql skill

bestPractices: Store database credentials securely in environment variables or .env files
Use --timeout flag for long-running analytical queries to prevent hanging
Use --tuples output mode when piping results to other scripts or tools
antiPatterns: Do not use this skill to query production databases without explicit authorization
Avoid executing untrusted SQL files without reviewing their contents first
Do not expose PG* environment variables in logs or error messages

faq.question: Which PostgreSQL versions are supported?
faq.answer: Any PostgreSQL version that works with the standard psql CLI client. The skill uses standard psql commands.

faq.question: What is the default query timeout?
faq.answer: The default timeout is 30 seconds. Use the --timeout option to increase this for long-running queries.

faq.question: Can I use this with cloud databases?
faq.answer: Yes. Set PGHOST, PGPORT, PGUSER, and PGPASSWORD to your cloud database endpoint credentials.

faq.question: Are my database credentials stored safely?
faq.answer: Credentials are read from environment variables only during query execution. Never hardcode passwords in queries.

faq.question: Why is my query timing out?
faq.answer: Complex queries or large datasets may exceed the 30-second default. Use --timeout 60000 for 60 seconds.

faq.question: How is this different from other database skills?
faq.answer: This skill uses the official psql CLI directly, supporting all PostgreSQL features and meta-commands without abstraction layers.
